description: A polygonal ballistic plate wrapped in a thin layer of nylon.  It combines a ceramic strike face with a polyethylene backer in order to achieve protection against even armor-piercing rifle rounds.  The ceramic breaks on impact, absorbing much of the damage, so while this plate is tough, it will wear out after a few hits.  Must be inserted into a plate carrier to be used.

variants.description:
The standard ballistic plate issued by the U.S. Army, ESAPI -- Enhanced Small Arms Protective Insert -- is a full-cut ballistic plate with a single-curve profile, composed of a boron carbide strike face, a UHMWPE backer, and a nylon cover.  This plate has been certified ESAPI, which exceeds the civilian NIJ IV rating and provides protection against armor-piercing rifle rounds including M855A1 and M80A1.  Plates like this one are intended to be inserted into a plate carrier, where it will absorb the energy of multiple rifle rounds before requiring replacement.  On the front, it says <color_yellow>
MEDIUM
STRIKE FACE


HANDLE WITH CARE</color>
On the back, starting from the top, it says <color_yellow>TOP
ESAPI - REV. G

U.S.</color>, as well as the name of the manufacturer -- Ceradyne, Inc. -- and its NSN.
